2605.20049.title: Does Code Cleanliness Affect Coding Agents? A Controlled Minimal-Pair Study
2605.20049.paper_summary: As autonomous coding agents see rapid adoption, their evaluation has primarily focused on task completion rates holding the target codebase fixed. This leaves a critical question unanswered: does the structural and stylistic quality, or ``cleanliness'' of the underlying code affect an agent's ability to navigate and modify it? To isolate the effect of code cleanliness from agent capability, we introduce an evaluation protocol built around minimal pairs: repositories that match on architecture, dependencies, and external behaviour, but differ on static-analysis rule violations and cognitive complexity. The pairs are constructed in both directions, by agent pipelines that either degrade a clean repository or clean a messy one. We author 33 tasks across six such pairs, evaluated through hidden tests at the application's public surface. Across 660 trials with Claude Code, code cleanliness does not change the agent's pass rate. However, it substantially alters the agent's operational footprint: agents working on cleaner code use 7 to 8% fewer tokens and reduce file revisitations by 34%. Our findings suggest that traditional maintainability principles remain highly relevant in the era of AI-driven development, shaping the computational cost and navigational efficiency of coding agents. Code cleanliness joins model choice, harness, and prompting as a factor that materially affects agent behaviours.
2605.20049.paper_summary_zh: 隨著自主編碼代理的快速採用，其評估主要集中在任務完成率上，並保持目標代碼庫不變。這留下了一個關鍵問題未得到解答：底層代碼的結構和風格質量，或稱為「整潔性」，是否影響代理的導航和修改能力？為了將代碼整潔性與代理能力的影響隔離，我們引入了一種基於最小對的評估協議：這些代碼庫在架構、依賴關係和外部行為上匹配，但在靜態分析規則違規和認知複雜性上存在差異。這些對是通過代理管道以雙向構建的，這些管道要麼降低一個乾淨代碼庫的質量，要麼清理一個雜亂的代碼庫。我們在六個這樣的對中創建了33個任務，通過應用程序的公共界面進行隱藏測試評估。在與Claude Code的660次試驗中，代碼整潔性並未改變代理的通過率。然而，它顯著改變了代理的操作足跡：在更整潔的代碼上工作的代理使用的標記少了7%到8%，並將文件重訪次數減少了34%。我們的研究結果表明，在人工智能驅動的開發時代，傳統的可維護性原則仍然高度相關，影響著編碼代理的計算成本和導航效率。代碼整潔性與模型選擇、駕馭和提示一起成為實質上影響代理行為的因素。
2605.20049.author: Priyansh Trivedi et.al.
